summary: Summary: The conversation begins with Speaker1 expressing anger about a friend's sudden invitation of an old flame; Speaker2 responds neutral at first but soon becomes frustrated and later angry while questioning Speaker1's judgment. Moving into marriage issues, Speaker1 remains angry and frustrated, insisting Joe won't marry and defending her own faithfulness, while Speaker2 stays neutral-to-frustrated and repeatedly tries to reason. On faith and belief, Speaker1, angry and frustrated, accuses Speaker2 of not noticing someone's absence, recounts a longer disappearance and says she will take her own life if Larry doesn't return—mixing anger, frustration, excited energy, and sadness as she urges Speaker2 not to stop believing. Speaker2 repeatedly attempts to calm Speaker1, grows frustrated by Speaker1's shaking and distress, and at one point becomes angry and shouts in surprise and frustration. The dialogue ends with Speaker1 still excited and sad about the possibility of return and Speaker2 left frustrated.
